title: Model Routing by Complexity
concept: Router pipelines evaluate user queries: route simple queries to cheap, fast models (like 8B) and complex queries to frontier models, cutting cost.

exercises.prompt: Why don't we just use the biggest, most powerful 'frontier' AI models for every single query?

options.text: They are always free to use.
They cost much more to run than smaller, simpler models.
They are faster than small models.

exercises.explain: Frontier models are highly capable but expensive. Small models are less capable but very fast and cheap. Routing balances the two.

cards.title: Stop Overpaying for Simple AI Tasks
cards.body: Imagine paying $10 every time you asked someone for the time of day. Sounds crazy, right? But that is exactly what happens when businesses use their most powerful, expensive AI models to answer simple greetings or basic questions. Let's learn how to route traffic and save big!

cards.title: What is Model Routing?
cards.body: Model routing by complexity is a system that inspects incoming user prompts and decides where to send them. Instead of sending every question to a giant, expensive AI, a 'router' evaluates the request. It sends easy tasks (like formatting text) to a small, cheap model, and saves the big, expensive model for difficult tasks (like logic puzzles or coding).

cards.title: The Customer Support Router
cards.body: Imagine you run a customer support line. When someone calls with a simple question like 'What are your hours?', a fast automated phone menu answers instantly. If a customer calls with a highly complex billing dispute, the call gets transferred to a senior human manager. Model routing does the exact same thing with AI: it keeps the simple questions fast and cheap, while saving the expensive brainpower for the hard stuff.

cards.title: Routing in Action
cards.body:
Let's look at two users chatting with your app:
• User A asks: 'Translate "Hello" to French.' The router detects this is easy and sends it to a fast 8B model. Cost: $0.0001.
• User B asks: 'Write a python script to simulate planetary orbits.' The router detects high complexity and sends it to a premium frontier model. Cost: $0.05.
By routing, you only pay for the premium model when you actually need it!

cards.title: Myth: Small Models Make Apps Worse
cards.body: Some developers think that using smaller models for simple tasks will make their app feel cheap or dumb. In reality, users often prefer it because smaller models respond much faster! For basic tasks, a routed app feels lightning-fast and works just as well.

cards.title: The Split Pipeline
cards.body: See how routing divides the workload to save resources while keeping quality high.

left.text: Easy queries (like 'Fix my grammar') go to small models. You pay pennies and get instant answers.

right.text: Complex queries (like 'Debug this database error') go to frontier models. You pay more, but only when necessary.

exercises.prompt: Where should a routing pipeline send a simple query like 'Correct the spelling of: receive'?

options.text: A small, fast, and inexpensive model (like an 8B parameter model)
The most expensive frontier model available
No model at all, the query should be rejected
A complex ensemble of ten different premium models

exercises.explain: Simple tasks like greeting a user or fixing typos do not need the reasoning power of a massive frontier model. A small, fast, and cheap model can handle them perfectly.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Model routing increases the total cost of running an AI application because you have to pay for two models at all times.
exercises.explain: Model routing actually reduces costs by offloading simple, high-volume tasks to cheaper models, reserving expensive models only for complex tasks.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of routing.
exercises.sentence: To save money, an AI pipeline uses a smart router to send easy prompts to small models and hard prompts to frontier models.
exercises.explain: A router sits at the entrance of your pipeline, evaluating the complexity of incoming prompts and sending them to the best-fit model.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes an AI system utilizing model routing?

options.text: An app that sends every single user message to the largest, most expensive model to guarantee quality.
An app that checks if a prompt is just a greeting, answers it with a fast 8B model, but routes math problems to a larger model.
An app that forces users to write in code so that the server doesn't have to process normal language.

exercises.explain: Option B represents a smart router that evaluates the text complexity and directs it to the appropriate model tier. Option A uses the premium model for everything (expensive), and Option C uses the cheap model for everything (poor quality on hard tasks).

exercises.prompt: You are designing a high-traffic AI app on a budget. Which strategy best describes how you would implement model routing to save costs?

options.text: It trains a single model to become much smaller and faster over time.
It uses a router to send simple queries to cheap models and hard queries to premium models.
It blocks all complex queries to keep operational costs at absolute zero.
It runs every single query through five different models at the exact same time.

exercises.explain: Model routing is a smart dispatching system. It inspects incoming queries and decides if they can be handled by a fast, low-cost model or if they require the advanced capabilities of a premium frontier model. This optimizes both speed and operational cost.
